subject: Re: errors during ts_etomo_patches and ts_reconstruct
body:
OK! Thanks! 
I will change my old OS to the newest Ubuntu now!

Reika 

On Thursday, July 24, 2025 at 3:37:40 PM UTC-7 alist...@gmail.com wrote:

> We added a feature which does not work with your old glibc and there is no 
> way to switch this feature off at runtime
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>
> On Jul 24, 2025, at 15:32, Reika Watanabe <reika.wa...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> ﻿Dear Alister,
>
>
> Thanks for your reply...! I understand. However, Warp_tools worked before 
> with this Linux...
> That does not make sense to me....
> Should I reinstall the Warp from the beginning?
>
> Reika 
>
>
>
> On Thursday, July 24, 2025 at 3:10:52 PM UTC-7 alist...@gmail.com wrote:
>
>> Hi Reika,
>>
>> glibc 2.17 was released in 2012, almost 15 years ago… sorry but we cannot 
>> support platforms like this, you would have to find your own solution in 
>> this case
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>>
>> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>>
>> On Jul 24, 2025, at 14:43, Reika Watanabe <reika.wa...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>> ﻿
>>
>> Dear Alister and others,
>>
>> I am using the newest version of warp with CentOS 7.9.
>>
>> # Name                    Version                   Build  Channel
>> warp                      2.0.0dev34              py311_0    warpem
>>
>> LSB Version: :core-4.1-amd64:core-4.1-ia32:core-4.1-noarch
>> Distributor ID: CentOS
>> Description: CentOS Linux release 7.9.2009 (Core)
>> Release: 7.9.2009
>> Codename: Core
>>
>> I cannot run ts_etomo_patches. (error message attached below)
>> Since I could not run ts_etomo_patches, I did ts_stack. I  got the error 
>> message (error message below) but at least I can find the tiltstack folder 
>> created and I can find aligned tilt. After manual alignment with Etomo, I 
>> imported alignment,  and  I tried to run ts_reconstruct. I again got the 
>> error message and reconstruction folder is empty.
>> After investigating error message with ChatGPT, it seems that my glibc 
>> version is too old. 2.17.
>> What do you recommend ?
>> Are there any way to avoid making png?
>>
>> WarpTools used to work well without error with this linux machine and I 
>> am using the same data set it used to work.
>> Thank you very much for your kind attention in advance.
>>  With best wishes,
>> Reika 
>>
>>
>> *ts_etomo_patches error messages*
>>
>> No alternative input specified, will use input parameters from 
>> warp_tiltseries.setting
>> File search will be relative to 
>> /mnt/staging/rwatanabe/DataProcess/Warp_tool/old_data_MARVdel_test/tomostar
>> 1 files found
>> Parsing previous results for each item, if available...
>> 1/1, previous metadata found for 0                                       
>>      
>> Connecting to workers...
>> Connected to 1 workers
>> Performing patch tracking alignment on all tilt-series...
>> Failed to process 
>> /mnt/staging/rwatanabe/DataProcess/Warp_tool/old_data_MARVdel_test/warp_tiltseries/TS_12.tomostar, 
>> marked as unselected
>> Check logs in 
>> /mnt/staging/rwatanabe/DataProcess/Warp_tool/old_data_MARVdel_test/warp_tiltseries/logs 
>> for more info.
>> Use the change_selection WarpTool to reactivate this item if required.
>> Exception details:
>> {"type":"Processing error","title":"Command execution 
>> failed","status":500,"detail":"System.TypeInitializationException: The type 
>> initializer for 'SkiaSharp.SKImageInfo' threw an exception.\n ---> 
>> System.DllNotFoundException: Unable to load shared library 'libSkiaSharp' 
>> or one of its dependencies. In order to help diagnose loading problems, 
>> consider using a tool like strace. If you're using glibc, consider setting 
>> the LD_DEBUG environment variable: 
>> \n/home/rwatanabe/mambaforge/envs/warp/bin/libSkiaSharp.so: cannot open 
>> shared object file: No such file or directory\n/lib64/libm.so.6: version 
>> `GLIBC_2.27' not found (required by 
>> /home/rwatanabe/mambaforge/envs/warp/bin/../lib/libSkiaSharp.so)\n/home/rwatanabe/mambaforge/envs/warp/bin/liblibSkiaSharp.so: 
>> cannot open shared object file: No such file or 
>> directory\n/home/rwatanabe/mambaforge/envs/warp/bin/libSkiaSharp: cannot 
>> open shared object file: No such file or 
>> directory\n/home/rwatanabe/mambaforge/envs/warp/bin/liblibSkiaSharp: cannot 
>> open shared object file: No such file or directory\n\n   at 
>> SkiaSharp.SkiaApi.sk_colortype_get_default_8888()\n   at 
>> SkiaSharp.SKImageInfo..cctor()\n   --- End of inner exception stack trace 
>> ---\n   at SkiaSharp.SKImageInfo..ctor(Int32 width, Int32 height, 
>> SKColorType colorType, SKAlphaType alphaType)\n   at 
>> SkiaSharp.SKBitmap..ctor(Int32 width, Int32 height, SKColorType colorType, 
>> SKAlphaType alphaType)\n   at Warp.Image.WritePNG(String path) in
